message:
fix(fj-lax): promote (U64, I32) to F64 in lattice promotion (frankenjax-13b6)

promote_dtype handled (U64, I64) by escaping to F64 (the documented
"no common integer type" rule — there is no signed integer wide enough
to represent the full unsigned range), but the (U64, I32) and (I32, U64)
pairs fell through to the catch-all I64 branch, returning I64. That is
unsound for the same reason: I64 cannot represent values above
i64::MAX which U64 routinely produces.

Extend the U64-with-signed branch from "I64" to "I32 | I64" so both
mixed-signedness widths escape to F64. Update the surrounding comment
to "U64 + I32/I64 → F64" so the rule is recorded next to the matrix.

Add two regression assertions in test_type_promotion_matrix_unsigned
for both directions: promote(U64, I32) and promote(I32, U64) must
both equal F64. Existing (U64, I64), (U32, U64), (U32, I64) cases
are kept as the surrounding context.

This is the JAX-parity follow-up to the i32 tensor dtype work tracked
under frankenjax-13b6 (extending tensor dtype promotion oracle to
i32) — the i32-on-tensor sweep would have surfaced this exact gap on
the next conformance run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
